description: set WARMDOWN_RATIO from 0.4 to 0.35 — more steps at peak LR
hypothesis: 0.4 helped; 0.35 gives even more peak-LR time in the 5-min budget
next_move: if better, try 0.3; if worse, 0.4 is the sweet spot

replacements.old: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.4    # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown
replacements.new: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.35   # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown

hypothesis: model may benefit from spending even less time in cooldown
next_move: if worse than 0.35 experiment, stop here

replacements.old: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.4    # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown
replacements.new: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.30   # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown

hypothesis: the downward trend in scalar LR may continue to help
next_move: if worse, 0.25 is the sweet spot

replacements.old: SCALAR_LR = 0.25        # learning rate for per-layer scalars (Adam)
replacements.new: SCALAR_LR = 0.20        # learning rate for per-layer scalars (Adam)

hypothesis: like SCALAR_LR, embeddings may converge better with a slightly gentler Adam step
next_move: if better, try 0.40; if worse, keep 0.45

replacements.old: EMBEDDING_LR = 0.45     # learning rate for token embeddings (Adam)
replacements.new: EMBEDDING_LR = 0.42     # learning rate for token embeddings (Adam)

hypothesis: a more aggressive embedding LR reduction may help after scalar and warmdown tuning
next_move: if worse than 0.42 experiment, use 0.42 as the new floor

replacements.old: EMBEDDING_LR = 0.45     # learning rate for token embeddings (Adam)
replacements.new: EMBEDDING_LR = 0.40     # learning rate for token embeddings (Adam)

hypothesis: 0.005 helped vs 0.004; lm_head may still benefit from a slightly larger Adam step
next_move: if worse, 0.005 is the sweet spot for unembedding

hypothesis: with calmer scalar/embedding/warmdown, Muon may benefit from a slightly larger step to match the new regime
next_move: if worse, keep 0.03; lowering was clearly bad so up is worth a try

replacements.old: MATRIX_LR = 0.03        # learning rate for matrix parameters (Muon)
replacements.new: MATRIX_LR = 0.032       # learning rate for matrix parameters (Muon)

commit_message: Lower weight decay to 0.15 on new best
description: lower WEIGHT_DECAY from 0.2 to 0.15 on the updated best checkpoint
hypothesis: the previous WD test was discarded, but the new LR/warmdown regime may be more tolerant of lighter regularization

replacements.old: WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.2      # cautious weight decay for Muon
replacements.new: WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.15     # cautious weight decay for Muon
